text: Let me check a few PRs to see the `closes_linear` field format:
Good, so `closes_linear` contains the Linear issue ID. Now let me filter PRs where the closed Linear issue's state is not 'done':

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input:
{"code":"(def prs-with-non-done-linear 
  (filter (fn [pr]
            (let [linear-id (:closes_linear pr)
                  linear-state (get linear-state-map linear-id)]
              (and linear-state (not= linear-state "done"))))
          prs-with-linear))
(count prs-with-non-done-linear)"}

       prs-with-non-done-linear))

(count result)"}
